Act Early.; labels are normalized without changing their substantive behavior. Operational definitions and video-scoring guidance are original project material. CDC materials are otherwise available from CDC at no charge.
disclaimer: This is not HELP content, a standardized assessment, a developmental screening tool, medical advice, or a CDC, HHS, Head Start, or WHO-endorsed product. It must not be used to diagnose a child or replace educator judgment and validated assessment practice.

creditDefinitions.label: Present
creditDefinitions.description: Direct recorded evidence satisfies the complete observable behavior and its required conditions.

creditDefinitions.label: Emerging
creditDefinitions.description: Direct recorded evidence shows a meaningful partial attempt, inconsistency, or additional support.

creditDefinitions.label: Not observed
creditDefinitions.description: A relevant opportunity and adequate response interval are recorded without the target behavior; mere absence is insufficient.

creditDefinitions.label: N/A
creditDefinitions.description: Visible context plus the governing rubric establish inapplicability; a missing opportunity is not N/A.

twoMinusRule.decisionReference: Disabled because CDC monitoring milestones do not define the licensed HELP developmental-age calculation rule.

skills.skillName: Calms down when spoken to or picked up
skills.sourceFramework: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early. developmental milestones
skills.observableDefinition: While fussing or crying, the target child noticeably settles — crying reduces or stops and the body relaxes — within a short interval after an adult speaks to them or picks them up.
observableIndicators: The child is visibly fussing or crying before the adult responds.
An adult speaks to the child or picks the child up, and that response is captured on the recording.
Within roughly 30 seconds the child's crying reduces or stops and the body visibly relaxes.
nonExamples: The child was already calm before the adult spoke or picked them up.
The child settles only after receiving a bottle, pacifier, or feeding rather than voice or holding.
Crying continues unchanged for the entire captured interval after the adult responds.
An adult or another child performs the behavior instead of the target child.
An adult reports that the behavior occurred outside the recorded observation.
A prompt or prepared opportunity is captured without a clearly attributable target-child response.
observationConditions: A fussing or crying episode and the adult's soothing response must both fall inside the recorded clip.
The relevant stimulus, request, demonstration, or opportunity must be visible or audible.
The target child's response and an adequate response interval must both be captured.
prohibitedInferences: Do not infer a diagnosis, developmental delay, intent, emotion, identity, or behavior outside the recorded observation.
Do not treat absence in a short video as evidence that the child cannot perform the behavior.

creditCriteria.present: Direct recorded evidence satisfies the complete observable definition for this skill under all listed observation conditions.
creditCriteria.emerging: The child briefly quiets or reduces crying when spoken to or picked up but does not settle fully during the captured interval.
creditCriteria.notObserved: A clear and relevant opportunity plus an adequate response interval are captured, but the target behavior is not observed. Absence elsewhere in the clip is insufficient.
creditCriteria.notApplicable: Use only when the visible context and the governing assessment rubric explicitly make this item inapplicable; age mismatch or missing opportunity alone is insufficient.

skills.skillName: Looks at your face
skills.sourceFramework: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early. developmental milestones
skills.observableDefinition: The target child orients their gaze toward a nearby adult's face and holds it there for at least a brief interval of roughly two seconds or more.
observableIndicators: An adult's face is within the infant's visual range, roughly arm's length away.
The child's eyes are open and gaze direction is clearly toward the adult's face.
Gaze steadies on the face rather than sweeping past it.
nonExamples: The child stares past the adult toward a light, window, or other high-contrast target.
The child's eyes are closed or the child is drowsy.
The child looks at a toy or object rather than a face.
An adult or another child performs the behavior instead of the target child.
An adult reports that the behavior occurred outside the recorded observation.
A prompt or prepared opportunity is captured without a clearly attributable target-child response.
observationConditions: The camera angle must allow the child's gaze direction and the adult's face position to be related.
The relevant stimulus, request, demonstration, or opportunity must be visible or audible.
The target child's response and an adequate response interval must both be captured.
prohibitedInferences: Do not infer a diagnosis, developmental delay, intent, emotion, identity, or behavior outside the recorded observation.
Do not treat absence in a short video as evidence that the child cannot perform the behavior.

creditCriteria.present: Direct recorded evidence satisfies the complete observable definition for this skill under all listed observation conditions.
creditCriteria.emerging: The child glances fleetingly at the adult's face for under two seconds without holding gaze.
creditCriteria.notObserved: A clear and relevant opportunity plus an adequate response interval are captured, but the target behavior is not observed. Absence elsewhere in the clip is insufficient.
creditCriteria.notApplicable: Use only when the visible context and the governing assessment rubric explicitly make this item inapplicable; age mismatch or missing opportunity alone is insufficient.

skills.skillName: Seems happy to see you when you walk up to them
skills.sourceFramework: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early. developmental milestones
skills.observableDefinition: As a familiar adult approaches into the child's view, the target child brightens: smiles, widens eyes, moves arms or legs with excitement, or vocalizes positively in response to the approach itself.
observableIndicators: The adult's approach into the child's field of view is captured.
The child's affect visibly brightens, time-locked to the approach.
The positive response is directed at the approaching person, not at an object.
nonExamples: The child brightens at a toy, bottle, or other object rather than the approaching person.
The child was already animated before the adult approached.
The response is produced by tickling or physical play rather than the approach.
An adult or another child performs the behavior instead of the target child.
An adult reports that the behavior occurred outside the recorded observation.
A prompt or prepared opportunity is captured without a clearly attributable target-child response.
observationConditions: The relevant stimulus, request, demonstration, or opportunity must be visible or audible.
The target child's response and an adequate response interval must both be captured.
prohibitedInferences: Do not infer a diagnosis, developmental delay, intent, emotion, identity, or behavior outside the recorded observation.
Do not treat absence in a short video as evidence that the child cannot perform the behavior.

creditCriteria.present: Direct recorded evidence satisfies the complete observable definition for this skill under all listed observation conditions.
creditCriteria.emerging: The child alerts and orients to the approaching adult without a clearly positive affect change.
creditCriteria.notObserved: A clear and relevant opportunity plus an adequate response interval are captured, but the target behavior is not observed. Absence elsewhere in the clip is insufficient.
creditCriteria.notApplicable: Use only when the visible context and the governing assessment rubric explicitly make this item inapplicable; age mismatch or missing opportunity alone is insufficient.

skills.skillName: Smiles when you talk to or smile at them
skills.sourceFramework: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early. developmental milestones
skills.observableDefinition: The target child produces a social smile in response to an adult talking to them or smiling at them.
observableIndicators: An adult directs talk or a smile at the child, and that engagement is captured.
The child's smile follows the adult's engagement within a few seconds.
The smile involves the whole face while the child attends to the adult.
nonExamples: A spontaneous or reflexive smile during drowsiness or sleep, unrelated to the adult's engagement.
A smile produced by tickling or other physical stimulation.
A smile at an object or event other than the engaging adult.
An adult or another child performs the behavior instead of the target child.
An adult reports that the behavior occurred outside the recorded observation.
A prompt or prepared opportunity is captured without a clearly attributable target-child response.
observationConditions: The relevant stimulus, request, demonstration, or opportunity must be visible or audible.
The target child's response and an adequate response interval must both be captured.
prohibitedInferences: Do not infer a diagnosis, developmental delay, intent, emotion, identity, or behavior outside the recorded observation.
Do not treat absence in a short video as evidence that the child cannot perform the behavior.

creditCriteria.present: Direct recorded evidence satisfies the complete observable definition for this skill under all listed observation conditions.
creditCriteria.emerging: The child's mouth corners move toward a partial smile after the adult engages, without a full social smile.
creditCriteria.notObserved: A clear and relevant opportunity plus an adequate response interval are captured, but the target behavior is not observed. Absence elsewhere in the clip is insufficient.
creditCriteria.notApplicable: Use only when the visible context and the governing assessment rubric explicitly make this item inapplicable; age mismatch or missing opportunity alone is insufficient.

skills.skillName: Makes sounds other than crying
skills.sourceFramework: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early. developmental milestones
skills.observableDefinition: The target child produces at least one vocal sound that is not crying or fussing, such as a coo, gurgle, short vowel sound, or contented grunt.
observableIndicators: A non-cry vocalization is audible on the recording.
The sound is attributable to the target child — the child's mouth movement is visible or the child is the only child present.
nonExamples: Crying, whimpering, or fussing sounds.
Vegetative sounds alone, such as hiccups, sneezes, burps, or coughs.
A voice from off-camera that cannot be attributed to the target child.
An adult or another child performs the behavior instead of the target child.
An adult reports that the behavior occurred outside the recorded observation.
A prompt or prepared opportunity is captured without a clearly attributable target-child response.
observationConditions: The target child and the complete behavior must be directly visible or audible without identity ambiguity.
prohibitedInferences: Do not infer a diagnosis, developmental delay, intent, emotion, identity, or behavior outside the recorded observation.
Do not treat absence in a short video as evidence that the child cannot perform the behavior.

creditCriteria.present: Direct recorded evidence satisfies the complete observable definition for this skill under all listed observation conditions.
creditCriteria.emerging: The child produces a single brief throaty sound of unclear quality that may not be a deliberate vocalization.
creditCriteria.notObserved: A clear and relevant opportunity plus an adequate response interval are captured, but the target behavior is not observed. Absence elsewhere in the clip is insufficient.
creditCriteria.notApplicable: Use only when the visible context and the governing assessment rubric explicitly make this item inapplicable; age mismatch or missing opportunity alone is insufficient.

skills.skillName: Reacts to loud sounds
skills.sourceFramework: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early. developmental milestones
skills.observableDefinition: Within a moment of a loud sound, the target child shows a visible change: startles, blinks, widens eyes, pauses movement, cries, or turns toward the sound.
observableIndicators: A loud sound is audible on the recording with an identifiable onset.
The child's behavior changes visibly within about two seconds of the sound.
The change is time-locked to the sound rather than to a visible event.
nonExamples: A reaction to a visible event that accompanied the sound, such as seeing a door swing open.
A movement that was already underway before the sound occurred.
No audible sound on the recording to which the reaction can be anchored.
An adult or another child performs the behavior instead of the target child.
An adult reports that the behavior occurred outside the recorded observation.
A prompt or prepared opportunity is captured without a clearly attributable target-child response.
observationConditions: The relevant stimulus, request, demonstration, or opportunity must be visible or audible.
The target child's response and an adequate response interval must both be captured.
prohibitedInferences: Do not infer a diagnosis, developmental delay, intent, emotion, identity, or behavior outside the recorded observation.
Do not treat absence in a short video as evidence that the child cannot perform the behavior.

creditCriteria.present: Direct recorded evidence satisfies the complete observable definition for this skill under all listed observation conditions.
creditCriteria.emerging: The child shows a subtle or delayed behavior change that cannot be confidently time-locked to the sound.
creditCriteria.notObserved: A clear and relevant opportunity plus an adequate response interval are captured, but the target behavior is not observed. Absence elsewhere in the clip is insufficient.
creditCriteria.notApplicable: Use only when the visible context and the governing assessment rubric explicitly make this item inapplicable; age mismatch or missing opportunity alone is insufficient.

skills.skillName: Watches you as you move
skills.sourceFramework: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early. developmental milestones
skills.observableDefinition: The target child's gaze follows a moving adult across part of the visual field, with head or eye movement tracking the adult's path.
observableIndicators: An adult moves across the child's field of view while the child is awake and alert.
The child's eyes or head visibly track the adult's movement for a portion of the path.
nonExamples: A fixed stare in one direction that happens to coincide with the adult's position.
Tracking a moving toy or object rather than a person.
The adult remains stationary, providing no movement to track.
An adult or another child performs the behavior instead of the target child.
An adult reports that the behavior occurred outside the recorded observation.
A prompt or prepared opportunity is captured without a clearly attributable target-child response.
observationConditions: The relevant stimulus, request, demonstration, or opportunity must be visible or audible.
The target child's response and an adequate response interval must both be captured.
prohibitedInferences: Do not infer a diagnosis, developmental delay, intent, emotion, identity, or behavior outside the recorded observation.
Do not treat absence in a short video as evidence that the child cannot perform the behavior.

creditCriteria.present: Direct recorded evidence satisfies the complete observable definition for this skill under all listed observation conditions.
creditCriteria.emerging: The child begins to track the moving adult but loses the target after a brief arc.
creditCriteria.notObserved: A clear and relevant opportunity plus an adequate response interval are captured, but the target behavior is not observed. Absence elsewhere in the clip is insufficient.
creditCriteria.notApplicable: Use only when the visible context and the governing assessment rubric explicitly make this item inapplicable; age mismatch or missing opportunity alone is insufficient.

skills.skillName: Looks at a toy for several seconds
skills.sourceFramework: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early. developmental milestones
skills.observableDefinition: The target child sustains visual attention on a toy or object for several seconds — roughly three seconds or more.
observableIndicators: A toy or object is within the child's visual range.
The child's gaze settles on the toy and holds for roughly three seconds or more.
nonExamples: Looking at a person's face rather than a toy.
An unfocused stare in the general direction of the toy.
A glance of under two seconds before gaze moves on.
An adult or another child performs the behavior instead of the target child.
An adult reports that the behavior occurred outside the recorded observation.
A prompt or prepared opportunity is captured without a clearly attributable target-child response.
observationConditions: The relevant stimulus, request, demonstration, or opportunity must be visible or audible.
The target child's response and an adequate response interval must both be captured.
prohibitedInferences: Do not infer a diagnosis, developmental delay, intent, emotion, identity, or behavior outside the recorded observation.
Do not treat absence in a short video as evidence that the child cannot perform the behavior.

creditCriteria.present: Direct recorded evidence satisfies the complete observable definition for this skill under all listed observation conditions.
creditCriteria.emerging: The child glances at the toy repeatedly without sustaining attention for several seconds.
creditCriteria.notObserved: A clear and relevant opportunity plus an adequate response interval are captured, but the target behavior is not observed. Absence elsewhere in the clip is insufficient.
creditCriteria.notApplicable: Use only when the visible context and the governing assessment rubric explicitly make this item inapplicable; age mismatch or missing opportunity alone is insufficient.

skills.skillName: Holds head up when on tummy
skills.sourceFramework: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early. developmental milestones
skills.observableDefinition: While lying on their tummy on a firm surface, the target child lifts their head and holds it up briefly without being propped.
observableIndicators: The child is positioned on their tummy on a firm surface.
The child lifts the head so the face comes off the surface, to roughly a 45-degree angle.
The lift is held briefly rather than immediately dropping.
nonExamples: Head control while held upright against an adult's shoulder.
The head is supported by a nursing pillow, rolled towel, or an adult's hand.
The child is reclined or on their back rather than on their tummy.
An adult or another child performs the behavior instead of the target child.
An adult reports that the behavior occurred outside the recorded observation.
A prompt or prepared opportunity is captured without a clearly attributable target-child response.
observationConditions: A tummy-time position on a firm surface must be captured in the clip.
The relevant stimulus, request, demonstration, or opportunity must be visible or audible.
The target child's response and an adequate response interval must both be captured.
prohibitedInferences: Do not infer a diagnosis, developmental delay, intent, emotion, identity, or behavior outside the recorded observation.
Do not treat absence in a short video as evidence that the child cannot perform the behavior.

creditCriteria.present: Direct recorded evidence satisfies the complete observable definition for this skill under all listed observation conditions.
creditCriteria.emerging: The child briefly bobs the head up during tummy time without holding the lift.
creditCriteria.notObserved: A clear and relevant opportunity plus an adequate response interval are captured, but the target behavior is not observed. Absence elsewhere in the clip is insufficient.
creditCriteria.notApplicable: Use only when the visible context and the governing assessment rubric explicitly make this item inapplicable; age mismatch or missing opportunity alone is insufficient.

skills.skillName: Moves both arms and both legs
skills.sourceFramework: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early. developmental milestones
skills.observableDefinition: During awake activity, the target child spontaneously moves both arms and both legs.
observableIndicators: Each of the four limbs is seen moving on its own at some point in the clip.
The movement is spontaneous and self-produced, not passive.
The limbs are unrestricted — the child is not swaddled or held.
nonExamples: An adult moving the child's limbs during play, dressing, or exercise.
The child is swaddled or bundled so limb movement cannot be assessed.
Only the limbs on one side are seen moving during the clip.
An adult or another child performs the behavior instead of the target child.
An adult reports that the behavior occurred outside the recorded observation.
A prompt or prepared opportunity is captured without a clearly attributable target-child response.
observationConditions: The target child and the complete behavior must be directly visible or audible without identity ambiguity.
prohibitedInferences: Do not infer a diagnosis, developmental delay, intent, emotion, identity, or behavior outside the recorded observation.
Do not treat absence in a short video as evidence that the child cannot perform the behavior.

creditCriteria.present: Direct recorded evidence satisfies the complete observable definition for this skill under all listed observation conditions.
creditCriteria.emerging: Spontaneous movement is visible but limited in range, or one limb's independent movement is not clearly captured.
creditCriteria.notObserved: A clear and relevant opportunity plus an adequate response interval are captured, but the target behavior is not observed. Absence elsewhere in the clip is insufficient.
creditCriteria.notApplicable: Use only when the visible context and the governing assessment rubric explicitly make this item inapplicable; age mismatch or missing opportunity alone is insufficient.

skills.skillName: Opens hands briefly
skills.sourceFramework: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early. developmental milestones
skills.observableDefinition: From the typical fisted resting posture, the target child spontaneously opens one or both hands briefly, extending the fingers.
observableIndicators: The child's hand is visible closely enough to see finger position.
The fingers extend from a fisted posture into an open hand, even momentarily.
The opening is spontaneous rather than produced by an adult.
nonExamples: An adult prying or stroking the fingers open.
Fingers spreading only during a startle response.
Hands that remain fisted throughout the visible portions of the clip.
An adult or another child performs the behavior instead of the target child.
An adult reports that the behavior occurred outside the recorded observation.
A prompt or prepared opportunity is captured without a clearly attributable target-child response.
observationConditions: The target child and the complete behavior must be directly visible or audible without identity ambiguity.
prohibitedInferences: Do not infer a diagnosis, developmental delay, intent, emotion, identity, or behavior outside the recorded observation.
Do not treat absence in a short video as evidence that the child cannot perform the behavior.

creditCriteria.present: Direct recorded evidence satisfies the complete observable definition for this skill under all listed observation conditions.
creditCriteria.emerging: The child partially extends some fingers without fully opening the hand.
creditCriteria.notObserved: A clear and relevant opportunity plus an adequate response interval are captured, but the target behavior is not observed. Absence elsewhere in the clip is insufficient.
creditCriteria.notApplicable: Use only when the visible context and the governing assessment rubric explicitly make this item inapplicable; age mismatch or missing opportunity alone is insufficient.

skills.skillName: Smiles on their own to get your attention
skills.sourceFramework: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early. developmental milestones
skills.observableDefinition: The target child spontaneously smiles while looking toward an adult to initiate engagement, without the adult having just smiled at, talked to, or touched the child.
observableIndicators: An adult is present and visible to the child but was not actively engaging the child in the moments before.
The child looks toward the adult and smiles as an initiation.
The smile is directed at the person, often with sustained gaze awaiting a response.
nonExamples: A smile elicited by the adult first smiling, talking, or tickling — that is the responsive smile expected by 2 months.
Smiling at a toy, mirror, or object rather than a person.
A drowsy or reflexive smile not directed at anyone.
An adult or another child performs the behavior instead of the target child.
An adult reports that the behavior occurred outside the recorded observation.
A prompt or prepared opportunity is captured without a clearly attributable target-child response.
observationConditions: An adult must be within the child's view without having prompted the child in the seconds before the smile.
The target child and the complete behavior must be directly visible or audible without identity ambiguity.
prohibitedInferences: Do not infer a diagnosis, developmental delay, intent, emotion, identity, or behavior outside the recorded observation.
Do not treat absence in a short video as evidence that the child cannot perform the behavior.

creditCriteria.present: Direct recorded evidence satisfies the complete observable definition for this skill under all listed observation conditions.
creditCriteria.emerging: The child looks toward the adult and smiles only after minimal adult engagement, or smiles without clearly directing it at the person.
creditCriteria.notObserved: A clear and relevant opportunity plus an adequate response interval are captured, but the target behavior is not observed. Absence elsewhere in the clip is insufficient.
creditCriteria.notApplicable: Use only when the visible context and the governing assessment rubric explicitly make this item inapplicable; age mismatch or missing opportunity alone is insufficient.

skills.skillName: Chuckles (not yet a full laugh) when you try to make them laugh
skills.sourceFramework: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early. developmental milestones
skills.observableDefinition: The target child produces an audible chuckle in response to an adult's playful attempt to amuse them.
observableIndicators: An adult's playful engagement — funny sounds, faces, or gentle play — is captured.
The child responds with an audible chuckle or breathy repeated vocal sound of amusement.
The chuckle is time-locked to the playful engagement.
nonExamples: A silent smile without any vocal component.
Vocal sounds not tied to the adult's playful engagement.
Squealing or crying rather than an amusement sound.
An adult or another child performs the behavior instead of the target child.
An adult reports that the behavior occurred outside the recorded observation.
A prompt or prepared opportunity is captured without a clearly attributable target-child response.
observationConditions: The relevant stimulus, request, demonstration, or opportunity must be visible or audible.
The target child's response and an adequate response interval must both be captured.
prohibitedInferences: Do not infer a diagnosis, developmental delay, intent, emotion, identity, or behavior outside the recorded observation.
Do not treat absence in a short video as evidence that the child cannot perform the behavior.

creditCriteria.present: Direct recorded evidence satisfies the complete observable definition for this skill under all listed observation conditions.
creditCriteria.emerging: The child responds with a broad smile and a single breathy sound that does not develop into a chuckle.
creditCriteria.notObserved: A clear and relevant opportunity plus an adequate response interval are captured, but the target behavior is not observed. Absence elsewhere in the clip is insufficient.
creditCriteria.notApplicable: Use only when the visible context and the governing assessment rubric explicitly make this item inapplicable; age mismatch or missing opportunity alone is insufficient.

skills.skillName: Looks at you, moves, or makes sounds to get or keep your attention
skills.sourceFramework: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early. developmental milestones
skills.observableDefinition: The target child directs gaze, body movement, or vocalization at an adult as a bid to obtain or hold the adult's attention.
observableIndicators: The child orients toward the adult while producing the bid — looking, wiggling, kicking, or vocalizing.
The bid intensifies when the adult's attention lapses or is directed elsewhere.
The child settles or brightens when the adult's attention returns.
nonExamples: Random movement or vocalization without orientation toward the adult.
Responses to the adult's own initiation rather than child-initiated bids.
Distress crying whose primary function is discomfort rather than social engagement.
An adult or another child performs the behavior instead of the target child.
An adult reports that the behavior occurred outside the recorded observation.
A prompt or prepared opportunity is captured without a clearly attributable target-child response.
observationConditions: The target child and the complete behavior must be directly visible or audible without identity ambiguity.
prohibitedInferences: Do not infer a diagnosis, developmental delay, intent, emotion, identity, or behavior outside the recorded observation.
Do not treat absence in a short video as evidence that the child cannot perform the behavior.

creditCriteria.present: Direct recorded evidence satisfies the complete observable definition for this skill under all listed observation conditions.
creditCriteria.emerging: The child briefly orients toward the adult with a single movement or sound but does not persist in the bid.
creditCriteria.notObserved: A clear and relevant opportunity plus an adequate response interval are captured, but the target behavior is not observed. Absence elsewhere in the clip is insufficient.
creditCriteria.notApplicable: Use only when the visible context and the governing assessment rubric explicitly make this item inapplicable; age mismatch or missing opportunity alone is insufficient.

skills.skillName: Makes sounds like 'oooo' and 'aahh' (cooing)
skills.sourceFramework: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early. developmental milestones
skills.observableDefinition: The target child produces open vowel sounds such as 'oooo' or 'aahh' — cooing vocal play.
observableIndicators: Sustained open vowel sounds are audible on the recording.
The sounds are attributable to the target child via visible mouth movement or a single-child context.
The vocalization has a relaxed, non-distressed quality.
nonExamples: Crying, fussing, or distress vocalizations.
Vegetative sounds such as grunts, coughs, or hiccups.
Consonant-vowel babbling strings such as 'bababa' — a later skill, not required here.
An adult or another child performs the behavior instead of the target child.
An adult reports that the behavior occurred outside the recorded observation.
A prompt or prepared opportunity is captured without a clearly attributable target-child response.
observationConditions: The target child and the complete behavior must be directly visible or audible without identity ambiguity.
prohibitedInferences: Do not infer a diagnosis, developmental delay, intent, emotion, identity, or behavior outside the recorded observation.
Do not treat absence in a short video as evidence that the child cannot perform the behavior.

creditCriteria.present: Direct recorded evidence satisfies the complete observable definition for this skill under all listed observation conditions.
creditCriteria.emerging: The child produces one brief vowel sound without sustained cooing.
creditCriteria.notObserved: A clear and relevant opportunity plus an adequate response interval are captured, but the target behavior is not observed. Absence elsewhere in the clip is insufficient.
creditCriteria.notApplicable: Use only when the visible context and the governing assessment rubric explicitly make this item inapplicable; age mismatch or missing opportunity alone is insufficient.

skills.skillName: Makes sounds back when you talk to them
skills.sourceFramework: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early. developmental milestones
skills.observableDefinition: After an adult talks directly to the target child, the child vocalizes within a few seconds in response.
observableIndicators: Adult speech directed at the child is audible on the recording.
The child vocalizes within a few seconds of the adult's utterance.
The child attends to the adult — gaze or body orientation — while responding.
nonExamples: Continuous vocalizing that ignores the adult's speech rather than responding to it.
A vocal response to a loud noise or object rather than to directed speech.
Mouth movements without any audible voice.
An adult or another child performs the behavior instead of the target child.
An adult reports that the behavior occurred outside the recorded observation.
A prompt or prepared opportunity is captured without a clearly attributable target-child response.
observationConditions: The relevant stimulus, request, demonstration, or opportunity must be visible or audible.
The target child's response and an adequate response interval must both be captured.
prohibitedInferences: Do not infer a diagnosis, developmental delay, intent, emotion, identity, or behavior outside the recorded observation.
Do not treat absence in a short video as evidence that the child cannot perform the behavior.

creditCriteria.notApplicable: Use only when the visible context and the governing assessment rubric explicitly make this item inapplicable; age mismatch or missing opportunity alone is insufficient.

skills.skillName: Turns head towards the sound of your voice
skills.sourceFramework: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early. developmental milestones
skills.observableDefinition: When an adult speaks from outside the target child's line of sight, the child turns their head or eyes toward the voice.
observableIndicators: An adult speaks from off to the side or out of the child's view, with the voice audible on the recording.
The child turns head or eyes toward the voice source within a few seconds.
The turn is time-locked to the voice onset.
nonExamples: Turning toward a speaker the child can already see — a visual cue, not sound localization.
Random head turning not time-locked to the voice.
Turning toward a loud non-voice sound — covered by the reaction-to-sounds item.
An adult or another child performs the behavior instead of the target child.
An adult reports that the behavior occurred outside the recorded observation.
A prompt or prepared opportunity is captured without a clearly attributable target-child response.
observationConditions: The relevant stimulus, request, demonstration, or opportunity must be visible or audible.
The target child's response and an adequate response interval must both be captured.
prohibitedInferences: Do not infer a diagnosis, developmental delay, intent, emotion, identity, or behavior outside the recorded observation.
Do not treat absence in a short video as evidence that the child cannot perform the behavior.

creditCriteria.present: Direct recorded evidence satisfies the complete observable definition for this skill under all listed observation conditions.
creditCriteria.emerging: The child shifts eyes toward the voice without turning the head.
creditCriteria.notObserved: A clear and relevant opportunity plus an adequate response interval are captured, but the target behavior is not observed. Absence elsewhere in the clip is insufficient.
creditCriteria.notApplicable: Use only when the visible context and the governing assessment rubric explicitly make this item inapplicable; age mismatch or missing opportunity alone is insufficient.

skills.skillName: Opens mouth at the sight of the breast or bottle when hungry
skills.sourceFramework: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early. developmental milestones
skills.observableDefinition: In a feeding context while hungry, the target child opens their mouth upon seeing the breast or bottle, before it touches the mouth.
observableIndicators: A feeding routine is underway or beginning, and the bottle or breast comes into the child's view.
The child opens their mouth in anticipation, before contact.
The response is tied to seeing the feeding source, not to touch.
nonExamples: A rooting response to a touch on the cheek or lips.

skills.skillName: Is shy, clingy, or fearful around strangers
skills.sourceFramework: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early. developmental milestones

The change is time-locked to the stranger's approach.
nonExamples: General fussiness present before and unrelated to the stranger.
Distress at a caregiver leaving — separation response, scored under the reaction-to-leaving item.
Wariness of a familiar educator or family member.
An adult or another child performs the behavior instead of the target child.
An adult reports that the behavior occurred outside the recorded observation.
A prompt or prepared opportunity is captured without a clearly attributable target-child response.
observationConditions: The submission context must identify the approaching person as unfamiliar to the child; the video alone cannot establish unfamiliarity.
The relevant stimulus, request, demonstration, or opportunity must be visible or audible.
The target child's response and an adequate response interval must both be captured.
prohibitedInferences: Do not infer a diagnosis, developmental delay, intent, emotion, identity, or behavior outside the recorded observation.
Do not treat absence in a short video as evidence that the child cannot perform the behavior.

A prompt or prepared opportunity is captured without a clearly attributable target-child response.

skills.skillName: Moves away from you but checks that you remain nearby
skills.sourceFramework: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early. developmental milestones
skills.observableDefinition: The target child moves away from their caregiver to explore, then spontaneously looks back toward the caregiver to check that they are still nearby.
observableIndicators: The child has space to move away from the caregiver and does so under their own initiative.
While away, the child turns to look back at the caregiver without being called.
The check-back is spontaneous and may repeat during exploration.
nonExamples: Wandering off without any look back toward the caregiver.
Looking back only because the caregiver called the child's name or spoke.
Remaining beside the caregiver for the entire clip — no separation to observe.
An adult or another child performs the behavior instead of the target child.
An adult reports that the behavior occurred outside the recorded observation.
A prompt or prepared opportunity is captured without a clearly attributable target-child response.
observationConditions: The caregiver and enough space for independent movement must both be present.
The relevant stimulus, request, demonstration, or opportunity must be visible or audible.
The target child's response and an adequate response interval must both be captured.
prohibitedInferences: Do not infer a diagnosis, developmental delay, intent, emotion, identity, or behavior outside the recorded observation.
Do not treat absence in a short video as evidence that the child cannot perform the behavior.

creditCriteria.present: Direct recorded evidence satisfies the complete observable definition for this skill under all listed observation conditions.
creditCriteria.emerging: The child moves away and glances back only when the caregiver speaks or makes a sound.
creditCriteria.notObserved: A clear and relevant opportunity plus an adequate response interval are captured, but the target behavior is not observed. Absence elsewhere in the clip is insufficient.
creditCriteria.notApplicable: Use only when the visible context and the governing assessment rubric explicitly make this item inapplicable; age mismatch or missing opportunity alone is insufficient.

skills.skillName: Points to show you something interesting
skills.sourceFramework: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early. developmental milestones
skills.observableDefinition: The target child points at an object or event and checks the adult's attention to share interest — a declarative point, not a request.
observableIndicators: A clear index-finger point at an object or event is visible.
The child alternates gaze between the pointed-at target and the adult's face, or vocalizes to draw attention.
The child is sharing interest — not trying to obtain the item or get help.
nonExamples: Pointing to request an object or help — the earlier 15-month requesting point.
Following an adult's point rather than producing one.
A reach with the whole hand toward a desired item.
An adult or another child performs the behavior instead of the target child.
An adult reports that the behavior occurred outside the recorded observation.
A prompt or prepared opportunity is captured without a clearly attributable target-child response.
observationConditions: The target child and the complete behavior must be directly visible or audible without identity ambiguity.
prohibitedInferences: Do not infer a diagnosis, developmental delay, intent, emotion, identity, or behavior outside the recorded observation.
Do not treat absence in a short video as evidence that the child cannot perform the behavior.

creditCriteria.present: Direct recorded evidence satisfies the complete observable definition for this skill under all listed observation conditions.
creditCriteria.emerging: The child points at the interesting target without checking the adult's attention.
creditCriteria.notObserved: A clear and relevant opportunity plus an adequate response interval are captured, but the target behavior is not observed. Absence elsewhere in the clip is insufficient.
creditCriteria.notApplicable: Use only when the visible context and the governing assessment rubric explicitly make this item inapplicable; age mismatch or missing opportunity alone is insufficient.

skills.skillName: Puts hands out for you to wash them
skills.sourceFramework: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early. developmental milestones
skills.observableDefinition: During a handwashing routine, the target child extends their hands toward the adult or water in cooperative anticipation of having them washed.
observableIndicators: A handwashing routine — sink, basin, wipes — is visible.
The child extends their hands toward the adult, water, or washing surface without being physically placed.
The extension anticipates or cooperates with the routine.
nonExamples: The adult grasping and positioning the child's hands.
The child playing in water without a washing routine.
Hands extended to reach for a toy or food rather than for washing.
An adult or another child performs the behavior instead of the target child.
An adult reports that the behavior occurred outside the recorded observation.
A prompt or prepared opportunity is captured without a clearly attributable target-child response.
observationConditions: The relevant routine, materials, assistance, and target-child response must be visible or audible.
Do not generalize from a different feeding, dressing, caregiving, or classroom context.
prohibitedInferences: Do not infer a diagnosis, developmental delay, intent, emotion, identity, or behavior outside the recorded observation.
Do not treat absence in a short video as evidence that the child cannot perform the behavior.

skills.skillName: Looks at a few pages in a book with you
skills.sourceFramework: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early. developmental milestones
skills.observableDefinition: During shared book time with an adult, the target child attends to several pages — looking at the pictures as pages turn, possibly patting or pointing at them.
observableIndicators: An adult shares a book with the child, turning through pages.
The child's gaze is on the book across at least a few page turns.
The child engages with the content — looking, patting, pointing, or vocalizing at pictures.
nonExamples: Mouthing, banging, or throwing the book without attending to pages.
The adult paging through while the child looks elsewhere.
A brief glance at a single page before leaving.
An adult or another child performs the behavior instead of the target child.
An adult reports that the behavior occurred outside the recorded observation.
A prompt or prepared opportunity is captured without a clearly attributable target-child response.
observationConditions: A shared book-reading interaction with an adult must occur in the clip.
The relevant stimulus, request, demonstration, or opportunity must be visible or audible.
The target child's response and an adequate response interval must both be captured.
prohibitedInferences: Do not infer a diagnosis, developmental delay, intent, emotion, identity, or behavior outside the recorded observation.
Do not treat absence in a short video as evidence that the child cannot perform the behavior.

skills.skillName: Helps with dressing by pushing an arm through a sleeve or lifting a foot
skills.sourceFramework: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early. developmental milestones
skills.observableDefinition: During a dressing routine, the target child actively assists — pushing an arm through a sleeve, lifting a foot for a shoe or pant leg, or extending a limb toward the garment.
observableIndicators: A dressing or undressing routine with an adult is visible.
The child produces an assisting movement — arm push, foot lift, leg extension — timed to the garment.
The assistance is the child's own movement, not the adult manipulating the limb.
nonExamples: The adult moving the child's limbs through the garment while the child is passive.
The child resisting or pulling away from dressing.
Limb movements unrelated to the garment's position.
An adult or another child performs the behavior instead of the target child.
An adult reports that the behavior occurred outside the recorded observation.
A prompt or prepared opportunity is captured without a clearly attributable target-child response.
observationConditions: The relevant routine, materials, assistance, and target-child response must be visible or audible.

skills.skillName: Tries to say three or more words besides mama or dada
skills.sourceFramework: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early. developmental milestones
skills.observableDefinition: Within the clip, the target child uses three or more different word approximations other than 'mama' or 'dada', each tied meaningfully to its referent or situation.
observableIndicators: At least three different words or word approximations are audible and attributable to the target child.
Each word connects to a referent or situation — naming, requesting, or commenting.
The words are distinct from one another, not repetitions of a single word.
nonExamples: Babble strings without referents.
Immediate echoes of words an adult just said.
Three repetitions of the same single word.
An adult or another child performs the behavior instead of the target child.
An adult reports that the behavior occurred outside the recorded observation.
A prompt or prepared opportunity is captured without a clearly attributable target-child response.
observationConditions: The target child and the complete behavior must be directly visible or audible without identity ambiguity.
prohibitedInferences: Do not infer a diagnosis, developmental delay, intent, emotion, identity, or behavior outside the recorded observation.
Do not treat absence in a short video as evidence that the child cannot perform the behavior.

skills.skillName: Follows a one-step direction without a gesture
skills.sourceFramework: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early. developmental milestones
skills.observableDefinition: The target child complies with a simple spoken one-step direction given without any gesture, gaze cue, or demonstration — for example 'Give it to me' with no outstretched hand.
observableIndicators: The adult's direction is audible and visibly free of accompanying gestures or pointing.
The child completes the requested action within a reasonable interval.
The action is not part of an automatic routine the child was already doing.
nonExamples: Directions accompanied by a gesture — the earlier 15-month gesture-plus-words skill.
Compliance with a routine the child performs anyway, such as sitting at an established snack time.
Physical guidance completing the action.
An adult or another child performs the behavior instead of the target child.
An adult reports that the behavior occurred outside the recorded observation.
A prompt or prepared opportunity is captured without a clearly attributable target-child response.
observationConditions: The relevant stimulus, request, demonstration, or opportunity must be visible or audible.
The target child's response and an adequate response interval must both be captured.
prohibitedInferences: Do not infer a diagnosis, developmental delay, intent, emotion, identity, or behavior outside the recorded observation.
Do not treat absence in a short video as evidence that the child cannot perform the behavior.

skills.skillName: Copies an adult doing a chore
skills.sourceFramework: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early. developmental milestones
skills.observableDefinition: After seeing an adult do a household or classroom chore, the target child imitates it — sweeping with a broom or brush, wiping a surface, or stirring a pot.
observableIndicators: An adult performs a chore within the child's view, or a recognizable chore imitation follows a known adult routine.
The child reproduces the chore action with an appropriate object.
The imitation captures the action's form — sweeping strokes, wiping motions — not just holding the tool.
nonExamples: Carrying or mouthing a broom or cloth without the chore action.
Complying with an instruction to sweep — direction-following rather than spontaneous imitation.
An adult guiding the child's hands through the motion.
An adult or another child performs the behavior instead of the target child.
An adult reports that the behavior occurred outside the recorded observation.
A prompt or prepared opportunity is captured without a clearly attributable target-child response.
observationConditions: The relevant stimulus, request, demonstration, or opportunity must be visible or audible.

skills.skillName: Uses a toy in a simple functional way
skills.sourceFramework: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early. developmental milestones
skills.observableDefinition: The target child plays with a toy according to its simple intended function — pushing a toy car along a surface, rocking a doll, or putting a shape in a shape sorter.
observableIndicators: A toy with a simple conventional use is available.
The child performs the toy's functional action, sustained or repeated.
The play is self-directed rather than a copy of an immediately preceding demonstration.
nonExamples: Mouthing, banging, or throwing the toy.
Watching another child or adult play with the toy.
Holding or carrying the toy without functional play.
An adult or another child performs the behavior instead of the target child.
An adult reports that the behavior occurred outside the recorded observation.
A prompt or prepared opportunity is captured without a clearly attributable target-child response.
observationConditions: A toy with a simple conventional function must be available to the child.

skills.skillName: Walks without holding on
skills.sourceFramework: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early. developmental milestones
skills.observableDefinition: The target child walks steadily across a room-scale distance without holding on to anyone or anything.
observableIndicators: The child walks a sustained stretch — several meters or across the play space.
No hands touch furniture, walls, push-toys, or people during the walk.
The gait is functional for moving between places, even if still wide-based.
nonExamples: A few isolated steps between supports — the earlier 15-month skill.
Cruising along furniture.
Walking with one hand held by an adult.
An adult or another child performs the behavior instead of the target child.
An adult reports that the behavior occurred outside the recorded observation.
A prompt or prepared opportunity is captured without a clearly attributable target-child response.
observationConditions: The target child and the complete behavior must be directly visible or audible without identity ambiguity.
prohibitedInferences: Do not infer a diagnosis, developmental delay, intent, emotion, identity, or behavior outside the recorded observation.

The child grips the tool and produces repeated marks.
The marking is self-produced, not hand-over-hand.

skills.sourceFramework: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early. developmental milestones
skills.observableDefinition: During a meal or snack, the target child holds an open, lidless cup and drinks from it by themselves, even if some liquid spills.
observableIndicators: A drinking routine with an open, lidless cup is visible.
The child holds the cup, brings it to the mouth, and drinks without an adult holding it.
Some spilling is acceptable and does not negate credit.
nonExamples: An adult holding the cup while the child drinks — the earlier 12-month skill.
Drinking from a sippy cup, straw cup, or bottle.
Playing with or pouring out the cup without drinking.
An adult or another child performs the behavior instead of the target child.
An adult reports that the behavior occurred outside the recorded observation.

The child repeatedly picks up food and eats it without adult delivery.
Finger feeding functions as the child's working method through the observed portion.
nonExamples: An adult placing each piece into the child's mouth.
A single successful piece with most attempts dropped — see the emerging boundary.
Smearing or throwing food without eating.
An adult or another child performs the behavior instead of the target child.

The child holds the spoon and attempts to scoop or dip.
The child brings the spoon toward their own mouth.

skills.skillName: Notices when another person is hurt or upset
skills.sourceFramework: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early. developmental milestones
skills.observableDefinition: When another person nearby is hurt or upset, the target child notices — pausing their activity, looking at the person with a sober or concerned expression, approaching, or commenting.
observableIndicators: Another person's visible or audible distress occurs in the clip.
The target child orients to the distressed person and pauses their own activity.
The child's expression sobers or the child approaches, touches, or comments.

Distress of their own triggered by the noise, without attention to the other person.

skills.skillName: Says at least two words together

skills.observableDefinition: The target child produces a spontaneous two-word combination expressing one idea, such as 'more milk' or 'daddy go'.
observableIndicators: A two-word utterance is audible and attributable to the target child.
The combination is generative — the child assembled it — rather than a memorized routine phrase.
The utterance fits its context meaningfully.
nonExamples: Single words, even many of them separately.
Rote or formulaic chunks such as 'thank you', 'all done', or song lyrics.
An immediate imitation of a two-word phrase an adult just modeled.
An adult or another child performs the behavior instead of the target child.
An adult reports that the behavior occurred outside the recorded observation.
A prompt or prepared opportunity is captured without a clearly attributable target-child response.
observationConditions: The target child and the complete behavior must be directly visible or audible without identity ambiguity.
prohibitedInferences: Do not infer a diagnosis, developmental delay, intent, emotion, identity, or behavior outside the recorded observation.
Do not treat absence in a short video as evidence that the child cannot perform the behavior.

creditCriteria.present: Direct recorded evidence satisfies the complete observable definition for this skill under all listed observation conditions.
creditCriteria.emerging: The child produces successive single words with a pause between them — 'mama ... milk' — without a combined utterance.
creditCriteria.notObserved: A clear and relevant opportunity plus an adequate response interval are captured, but the target behavior is not observed. Absence elsewhere in the clip is insufficient.
creditCriteria.notApplicable: Use only when the visible context and the governing assessment rubric explicitly make this item inapplicable; age mismatch or missing opportunity alone is insufficient.

skills.skillName: Points to at least two body parts when asked
skills.sourceFramework: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early. developmental milestones
skills.observableDefinition: When an adult asks the target child to show body parts by name, without modeling, the child correctly points to at least two different ones.
observableIndicators: The adult's requests are audible and name the body parts without pointing to or touching them.
The child points to or touches the correct body part on themselves, a doll, or another person.
At least two different body parts are correctly identified.
nonExamples: The adult touching or pointing at the body part first — the child copies the model.
Responses drilled in an immediately preceding rehearsal of the same items.
One correct body part only — see the emerging boundary.
An adult or another child performs the behavior instead of the target child.
An adult reports that the behavior occurred outside the recorded observation.
A prompt or prepared opportunity is captured without a clearly attributable target-child response.
observationConditions: The relevant stimulus, request, demonstration, or opportunity must be visible or audible.
The target child's response and an adequate response interval must both be captured.
prohibitedInferences: Do not infer a diagnosis, developmental delay, intent, emotion, identity, or behavior outside the recorded observation.
Do not treat absence in a short video as evidence that the child cannot perform the behavior.

creditCriteria.present: Direct recorded evidence satisfies the complete observable definition for this skill under all listed observation conditions.
creditCriteria.emerging: The child correctly identifies one named body part but not a second.
creditCriteria.notObserved: A clear and relevant opportunity plus an adequate response interval are captured, but the target behavior is not observed. Absence elsewhere in the clip is insufficient.
creditCriteria.notApplicable: Use only when the visible context and the governing assessment rubric explicitly make this item inapplicable; age mismatch or missing opportunity alone is insufficient.

skills.skillName: Uses gestures beyond waving and pointing
skills.sourceFramework: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early. developmental milestones
skills.observableDefinition: The target child uses at least one communicative gesture other than waving or pointing — such as blowing a kiss, nodding yes, shaking the head no, or a shrug.
observableIndicators: A gesture other than a wave or point is visible and clearly communicative.
The gesture is directed at a person or used to answer or comment.
The gesture is the child's own production, not physically prompted.
nonExamples: Waving or pointing — already covered by earlier items.
Motions copied within a song or fingerplay routine as imitation.
An adult moving the child's hands into the gesture.
An adult or another child performs the behavior instead of the target child.
An adult reports that the behavior occurred outside the recorded observation.
A prompt or prepared opportunity is captured without a clearly attributable target-child response.
observationConditions: The target child and the complete behavior must be directly visible or audible without identity ambiguity.
prohibitedInferences: Do not infer a diagnosis, developmental delay, intent, emotion, identity, or behavior outside the recorded observation.
Do not treat absence in a short video as evidence that the child cannot perform the behavior.

creditCriteria.present: Direct recorded evidence satisfies the complete observable definition for this skill under all listed observation conditions.
creditCriteria.emerging: The child partially attempts such a gesture — puckering toward a blown kiss without the blow, or an ambiguous head movement.
creditCriteria.notObserved: A clear and relevant opportunity plus an adequate response interval are captured, but the target behavior is not observed. Absence elsewhere in the clip is insufficient.
creditCriteria.notApplicable: Use only when the visible context and the governing assessment rubric explicitly make this item inapplicable; age mismatch or missing opportunity alone is insufficient.

skills.skillName: Holds an object in one hand while the other hand works it
skills.sourceFramework: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early. developmental milestones
skills.observableDefinition: The target child stabilizes an object with one hand while the other hand performs a different action on it — holding a container while pulling its lid off, or steadying paper while marking it.
observableIndicators: An object affording two-handed complementary use is available.
One hand holds or stabilizes while the other manipulates — the hands do different jobs.
The coordinated action achieves its purpose, such as the lid coming off.
nonExamples: Both hands doing the same action, such as holding one cup with two hands.
An adult stabilizing the object while the child manipulates.
Holding two unrelated objects, one per hand, without coordination.
An adult or another child performs the behavior instead of the target child.
An adult reports that the behavior occurred outside the recorded observation.
A prompt or prepared opportunity is captured without a clearly attributable target-child response.
observationConditions: An object that requires or invites two-handed complementary use must be available.
The relevant stimulus, request, demonstration, or opportunity must be visible or audible.
The target child's response and an adequate response interval must both be captured.
prohibitedInferences: Do not infer a diagnosis, developmental delay, intent, emotion, identity, or behavior outside the recorded observation.
Do not treat absence in a short video as evidence that the child cannot perform the behavior.

creditCriteria.present: Direct recorded evidence satisfies the complete observable definition for this skill under all listed observation conditions.
creditCriteria.emerging: The child attempts the two-handed coordination but loses grip or abandons the manipulation.
creditCriteria.notObserved: A clear and relevant opportunity plus an adequate response interval are captured, but the target behavior is not observed. Absence elsewhere in the clip is insufficient.
creditCriteria.notApplicable: Use only when the visible context and the governing assessment rubric explicitly make this item inapplicable; age mismatch or missing opportunity alone is insufficient.

skills.skillName: Tries switches, knobs, or buttons on a toy
skills.sourceFramework: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early. developmental milestones
skills.observableDefinition: With a toy that has switches, knobs, buttons, or dials, the target child deliberately operates or attempts to operate the mechanisms.
observableIndicators: A toy with operable mechanisms is available.
The child presses, turns, flips, or slides the mechanism purposefully.
The child attends to the effect — looking for the light, sound, or movement produced.
nonExamples: Banging, shaking, or mouthing the toy without operating its mechanisms.
Watching an adult or another child operate the toy.
Accidental activation while carrying the toy.
An adult or another child performs the behavior instead of the target child.
An adult reports that the behavior occurred outside the recorded observation.
A prompt or prepared opportunity is captured without a clearly attributable target-child response.
observationConditions: A toy with switches, knobs, buttons, or similar mechanisms must be available.
The relevant stimulus, request, demonstration, or opportunity must be visible or audible.
The target child's response and an adequate response interval must both be captured.
prohibitedInferences: Do not infer a diagnosis, developmental delay, intent, emotion, identity, or behavior outside the recorded observation.
Do not treat absence in a short video as evidence that the child cannot perform the behavior.

creditCriteria.present: Direct recorded evidence satisfies the complete observable definition for this skill under all listed observation conditions.
creditCriteria.emerging: The child touches or pokes at the mechanism without a working operation attempt.
creditCriteria.notObserved: A clear and relevant opportunity plus an adequate response interval are captured, but the target behavior is not observed. Absence elsewhere in the clip is insufficient.
creditCriteria.notApplicable: Use only when the visible context and the governing assessment rubric explicitly make this item inapplicable; age mismatch or missing opportunity alone is insufficient.

skills.skillName: Combines more than one toy in the same play activity
skills.sourceFramework: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early. developmental milestones
skills.observableDefinition: The target child relates two or more toys within one play scheme — putting toy food on a toy plate, loading blocks into a truck, or feeding a doll with a toy spoon.
observableIndicators: Multiple toys are available to the child.
The child combines the toys so that one acts on or with the other within a single play idea.
The combination is purposeful and sustained beyond momentary contact.
nonExamples: Holding two toys, one in each hand, without relating them.
Playing with toys one after another in separate schemes.
Stacking or container play scored under earlier construction items.
An adult or another child performs the behavior instead of the target child.
An adult reports that the behavior occurred outside the recorded observation.
A prompt or prepared opportunity is captured without a clearly attributable target-child response.
observationConditions: At least two combinable toys must be available to the child.
The relevant stimulus, request, demonstration, or opportunity must be visible or audible.
The target child's response and an adequate response interval must both be captured.
prohibitedInferences: Do not infer a diagnosis, developmental delay, intent, emotion, identity, or behavior outside the recorded observation.
Do not treat absence in a short video as evidence that the child cannot perform the behavior.

creditCriteria.present: Direct recorded evidence satisfies the complete observable definition for this skill under all listed observation conditions.
creditCriteria.emerging: The child briefly brings the toys together in a related way without sustaining the combined play.
creditCriteria.notObserved: A clear and relevant opportunity plus an adequate response interval are captured, but the target behavior is not observed. Absence elsewhere in the clip is insufficient.
creditCriteria.notApplicable: Use only when the visible context and the governing assessment rubric explicitly make this item inapplicable; age mismatch or missing opportunity alone is insufficient.

skills.skillName: Kicks a ball
skills.sourceFramework: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early. developmental milestones
skills.observableDefinition: The target child kicks a ball forward with a swinging leg motion while standing, without holding on for support.
observableIndicators: A ball is available on the ground near the child.
The child swings a leg and contacts the ball, sending it forward.
The child maintains standing balance through the kick without holding support.
nonExamples: Walking into the ball so it rolls incidentally.
Throwing or pushing the ball with hands.
Kicking while holding an adult's hand or furniture for balance.
An adult or another child performs the behavior instead of the target child.
An adult reports that the behavior occurred outside the recorded observation.
A prompt or prepared opportunity is captured without a clearly attributable target-child response.
observationConditions: A kickable ball must be available on the ground.
The relevant stimulus, request, demonstration, or opportunity must be visible or audible.
The target child's response and an adequate response interval must both be captured.
prohibitedInferences: Do not infer a diagnosis, developmental delay, intent, emotion, identity, or behavior outside the recorded observation.
Do not treat absence in a short video as evidence that the child cannot perform the behavior.

creditCriteria.present: Direct recorded evidence satisfies the complete observable definition for this skill under all listed observation conditions.
creditCriteria.emerging: The child steps at the ball and nudges it forward with the foot without a swinging kick.
creditCriteria.notObserved: A clear and relevant opportunity plus an adequate response interval are captured, but the target behavior is not observed. Absence elsewhere in the clip is insufficient.
creditCriteria.notApplicable: Use only when the visible context and the governing assessment rubric explicitly make this item inapplicable; age mismatch or missing opportunity alone is insufficient.

skills.skillName: Runs
skills.sourceFramework: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early. developmental milestones
skills.observableDefinition: The target child runs — a clearly faster-than-walking gait with quick alternating steps and a moment where both feet leave the ground.
observableIndicators: The child moves at a pace clearly faster than their walk.
Steps alternate quickly with brief flight moments, distinguishing running from fast walking.
The child maintains control without immediately falling.
nonExamples: Fast stiff-legged walking without any flight phase.
A few hurried steps while held by the hand.
Stumbling forward momentum ending in a fall.
An adult or another child performs the behavior instead of the target child.
An adult reports that the behavior occurred outside the recorded observation.
A prompt or prepared opportunity is captured without a clearly attributable target-child response.
observationConditions: The target child and the complete behavior must be directly visible or audible without identity ambiguity.
prohibitedInferences: Do not infer a diagnosis, developmental delay, intent, emotion, identity, or behavior outside the recorded observation.
Do not treat absence in a short video as evidence that the child cannot perform the behavior.

creditCriteria.present: Direct recorded evidence satisfies the complete observable definition for this skill under all listed observation conditions.
creditCriteria.emerging: The child produces a hurried, stiff trot that does not clearly leave the ground.
creditCriteria.notObserved: A clear and relevant opportunity plus an adequate response interval are captured, but the target behavior is not observed. Absence elsewhere in the clip is insufficient.
creditCriteria.notApplicable: Use only when the visible context and the governing assessment rubric explicitly make this item inapplicable; age mismatch or missing opportunity alone is insufficient.

skills.skillName: Walks (not crawls) up a few stairs with or without help
skills.sourceFramework: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early. developmental milestones
skills.observableDefinition: The target child walks upright up a few stairs — stepping with feet, not crawling on hands and knees — with or without holding a rail or hand.
observableIndicators: Stairs or steps are present in the environment.
The child ascends at least two or three steps upright on their feet.
Holding a rail, wall, or adult hand is acceptable; hands-and-knees climbing is not.
nonExamples: Crawling up the stairs on hands and knees.
Being carried or substantially lifted between steps.
Standing at the bottom stair without ascending.
An adult or another child performs the behavior instead of the target child.
An adult reports that the behavior occurred outside the recorded observation.
A prompt or prepared opportunity is captured without a clearly attributable target-child response.
observationConditions: Stairs or steps must be present and accessible in the clip.
The relevant stimulus, request, demonstration, or opportunity must be visible or audible.
The target child's response and an adequate response interval must both be captured.
prohibitedInferences: Do not infer a diagnosis, developmental delay, intent, emotion, identity, or behavior outside the recorded observation.
Do not treat absence in a short video as evidence that the child cannot perform the behavior.

creditCriteria.present: Direct recorded evidence satisfies the complete observable definition for this skill under all listed observation conditions.
creditCriteria.emerging: The child walks up one step with heavy support and then reverts to crawling.
creditCriteria.notObserved: A clear and relevant opportunity plus an adequate response interval are captured, but the target behavior is not observed. Absence elsewhere in the clip is insufficient.
creditCriteria.notApplicable: Use only when the visible context and the governing assessment rubric explicitly make this item inapplicable; age mismatch or missing opportunity alone is insufficient.

skills.skillName: Eats with a spoon
skills.sourceFramework: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early. developmental milestones
skills.observableDefinition: During a meal, the target child eats with a spoon as a working method — scooping food and getting it to their mouth with reasonable success and limited spilling.
observableIndicators: A meal routine with a spoon and scoopable food is visible.
The child scoops and delivers food to the mouth repeatedly, mostly successfully.
The spoon is the child's working feeding method through the observed portion.
nonExamples: An adult loading the spoon or guiding the child's hand.
Isolated attempts with most food lost — the earlier 18-month trying-to-use skill.
Eating exclusively with fingers while the spoon lies unused.
An adult or another child performs the behavior instead of the target child.
An adult reports that the behavior occurred outside the recorded observation.
A prompt or prepared opportunity is captured without a clearly attributable target-child response.
observationConditions: The relevant routine, materials, assistance, and target-child response must be visible or audible.
Do not generalize from a different feeding, dressing, caregiving, or classroom context.
prohibitedInferences: Do not infer a diagnosis, developmental delay, intent, emotion, identity, or behavior outside the recorded observation.
Do not treat absence in a short video as evidence that the child cannot perform the behavior.

creditCriteria.present: Direct recorded evidence satisfies the complete observable definition for this skill under all listed observation conditions.
creditCriteria.emerging: The child uses the spoon with frequent spills and mixes in finger feeding to compensate.
creditCriteria.notObserved: A clear and relevant opportunity plus an adequate response interval are captured, but the target behavior is not observed. Absence elsewhere in the clip is insufficient.
creditCriteria.notApplicable: Use only when the visible context and the governing assessment rubric explicitly make this item inapplicable; age mismatch or missing opportunity alone is insufficient.

skills.skillName: Plays next to other children and sometimes with them
skills.sourceFramework: CDC Learn the Signs. Act Early. developmental milestones
skills.observableDefinition: The target child plays alongside other children and, at least once, engages with them — sharing materials, joining an action, or exchanging a play turn.
observableIndicators: Other children are present and playing near the target child.
The target child plays in proximity to peers, oriented to the shared space.
At least one interactive exchange occurs — handing or taking materials cooperatively, imitating within a shared game, or acting jointly.
nonExamples: Playing alone at a distance from other children.
Interactions that consist only of grabbing or conflict without play exchange.
Exchanges entirely staged and sustained by an adult moving materials between children.
An adult or another child performs the behavior instead of the target child.
An adult reports that the behavior occurred outside the recorded observation.
A prompt or prepared opportunity is captured without a clearly attributable target-child response.
observationConditions: Other children must be present and engaged in play near the target child.
The relevant stimulus, request, demonstration, or opportunity must be visible or audible.
The target child's response and an adequate response interval must both be captured.
prohibitedInferences: Do not infer a diagnosis, developmental delay, intent, emotion, identity, or behavior outside the recorded observation.
Do not treat absence in a short video as evidence that the child cannot perform the behavior.

creditCriteria.present: Direct recorded evidence satisfies the complete observable definition for this skill under all listed observation conditions.

creditCriteria.notObserved: A clear and relevant opportunity plus an adequate response interval are captured, but the target behavior is not observed. Absence elsewhere in the clip is insufficient.
